title: GTM → Portfolio full journal sync
summary: Implemented phoneHomeToPortfolio() so child GTMs automatically sync full journal content up to their portfolio parent on session end.
detail: Added GTMSyncPayload interface and phoneHomeToPortfolio() function to src/lib/service-gtm.ts. Mirrors the existing phoneHomeToGTM() pattern: reads GTM journal entries since last sync, copies journal files to portfolio with gtm-{name}- prefix, writes aggregated service-sync journal entry to portfolio, updates registered_services last_sync, writes inbox trigger, emits context hub event (non-fatal). Upgraded jfl portfolio phone-home command (src/commands/portfolio.ts) to call phoneHomeToPortfolio() instead of the old lightweight report. Added phone_home_to_portfolio helper to session-cleanup.sh with two call sites (after both successful merge+push paths). Added portfolio sync block to session-end.sh between the push and product sync steps. Build passes cleanly.
next: Test with a real child GTM that has portfolio_parent set

title: visa-portfolio: superhero command center initialized
summary: Built Andrew's master Visa coordination layer at /Users/andrewhathaway/code/visa. Portfolio type with port 4300, visa-superhero and productrank-gtm registered, 13 knowledge files populated including canonical intel extracted from visa-superhero.
detail: Created from scratch: git init, .jfl/config.json (portfolio type, port 4300), .mcp.json (port 4300), .gitignore (excludes all 6 nested workspace repos), CLAUDE.md (custom superhero command center doc, ~7KB). Extracted real knowledge from visa-superhero: VISION, NARRATIVE, THESIS, TEAM, INTEL, PROJECTS, visa-ecosystem, conversations/. Created new portfolio-only files: WORKSPACE_MAP (full path/port/purpose map), ANDREW (Andrew's brief for agents), OPPORTUNITIES (8 prioritized opportunities including CyberSource shell, virtual card MCP, Visa skill files), DECISIONS (master log: DX-001 CyberSource, DX-002 virtual card, TEAM-001 Colorado telecommute, ARCH-001 TAP vs VI, AGENT-001 Allium pattern, CRYPTO-001 x402/USDC), METRICS (ProductRank baselines 0.845/0.844, port map, team context). Ran jfl portfolio register for both GTMs - portfolio_parent backlink written to child configs. Initial commit: 135 files. Git status clean (subdirectories correctly excluded by .gitignore).
next: Open Claude Code in /Users/andrewhathaway/code/visa — the portfolio is ready to use

title: JFL-CLI → Pi migration: packages/pi full implementation
summary: Implemented the complete @jfl/pi package as described in the Pi migration plan. All phases 1-8 implemented in a single session: foundation package, all extensions, skills migration, themes, teams, and CLI integration.
detail: Created packages/pi/ with full structure: (1) package.json with pi-package keyword, tsconfig.json; (2) types.ts — Pi extension type definitions (PiContext, PiLifecycleHooks, PiTool, etc.); (3) index.ts — master entry composing all sub-extensions; (4) session.ts — worktree lifecycle, auto-commit daemon, shell script delegation; (5) map-bridge.ts — Pi lifecycle → MAP event bus HTTP posts with scope enforcement (reads context_scope from .jfl/config.json); (6) context.ts — hub ensure, before_agent_start injection, jfl_context tool; (7) journal.ts — git commit detection, /journal command, footer widget, before_compact check; (8) eval.ts — per-turn eval capture via eval-store.ts; (9) hud-tool.ts — jfl_hud tool, /hud command, aboveEditor widget; (10) crm-tool.ts — delegates to ./crm CLI; (11) memory-tool.ts — Context Hub memory search API; (12) synopsis-tool.ts — synopsis CLI delegation; (13) stratus-bridge.ts — predict before agent_start, resolve after agent_end, training tuples to ~/.jfl/training-buffer.jsonl; (14) peter-parker.ts — full orchestrator replacing WebSocket bridge, subscribes to MAP events, spawns Pi RPC subprocesses, review loop max 5 iterations; (15) portfolio-bridge.ts — phoneHomeToPortfolio on shutdown, journal sync to parent, eval propagation to portfolio hub; (16) agent-grid.ts — /grid command, polls agent:health, live status display. Skills: copied 17 existing skills + created 3 new (pi-agents, eval, context), renamed ralph-tui→orchestrate, updated hud/end/orchestrate for Pi. Themes: jfl.theme.json with 51 design tokens. Teams: gtm-team.yaml (Scout/Planner/Builder/GTM/Content/Eval) and dev-team.yaml. CLI: jfl pi + jfl pi agents run commands. AGENTS.md coordination docs. All TypeScript compiles clean, jfl build passes.
next: Test with actual Pi installation. Wire agent:health emitter into each spawned subprocess. Possibly add nightly cron for training-buffer→Stratus policy head batch in stratus-bridge.ts. Publish as @jfl/pi to npm.
learned: Pi extension API uses named lifecycle hooks (session_start, agent_start, etc.) — clean integration points vs Claude Code hooks
Pi's before_agent_start can return systemPromptAddition for context injection without MCP
scope enforcement in map-bridge is simple: read context_scope from config, filter produces/consumes/denied patterns

title: Policy head trainer + inference layer verified end-to-end
summary: Built numpy-based Q-network trainer (1536→256→256→1) using Stratus embeddings, and TypeScript inference layer. First training on 540 mined tuples achieved rank correlation 0.7885. Inference verified: loads weights, embeds via Stratus API, forward pass produces clean ranked outputs.
detail: train-policy-head.py: Adam optimizer with He/Kaiming init (0.1 scale), gradient clipping (individual [-5,5], global norm 1.0, update [-0.1,0.1]), float64 throughout (critical fix — float32 overflowed). Caches embeddings to .jfl/policy-embeddings.json (16MB, 540 entries). 90/10 train/val split, best epoch 51/200. policy-head.ts: PolicyHeadInference class with embedText(), predictReward(), rankActions(). Forward pass uses matVecMulTransposed for correct weight orientation. eval.ts: added `jfl eval train` command that spawns Python trainer. Inference test showed model correctly separates candidate actions by predicted reward.
incomplete: Wire policy head into autoresearch action selection
Daily retrain cron when new tuples accumulate
next: Integrate PolicyHeadInference into autoresearch loop so Peter Parker uses learned preferences to select experiments
learned: float64 is mandatory for 1536-dim inputs — float32 overflows even with gradient clipping
Rank correlation (0.79) matters more than direction accuracy (0.46) — the model learns relative ordering well
Stratus embedding caching saves ~$0.27/retrain by avoiding re-embedding unchanged entries

title: Pi CLI flag fixes: --skill, --theme path, journal footer
summary: Fixed three issues in jfl pi command: (1) --skills→--skill typo caused skills path to be sent as initial prompt to agent, triggering 'already processing' error and dumping all 22 skills as response; (2) --theme 'jfl' resolved to nonexistent path, added findPiTheme() to resolve actual jfl.theme.json path; (3) moved journal nudge from aboveEditor widget to setStatus footer for less intrusive UX.
detail: Root cause of the main bug: Pi CLI uses --skill (singular) not --skills. The unrecognized flag caused Pi to treat the skills directory path as a positional argument (initial user prompt). The agent started processing this 'prompt' (listing the directory), and when the extension's before_agent_start hook fired during that, it hit 'Agent is already processing a prompt' error. Also resolved the DU merge conflict on .jfl/map-events.jsonl that was blocking commits.
learned: Pi --theme expects a file path, not a theme name
Unrecognized Pi CLI flags become positional args (initial prompt)

title: Full Pi TUI upgrade: footer, tool renderers, overlays, shortcuts, notifications, bookmarks
summary: Massive UX/DX upgrade to @jfl/pi leveraging Pi's full TUI capabilities. Added custom footer with live telemetry, themed renderCall/renderResult for all 11 JFL tools, keyboard shortcuts with floating overlays, native terminal notifications, session bookmarks, and expanded PiContext shim exposing full Pi API.
detail: Deep research into Pi's TUI system revealed we were using ~6 of ~20 available capabilities. Built 5 new extension files (footer.ts, shortcuts.ts, notifications.ts, bookmarks.ts, tool-renderers.ts) and significantly upgraded 9 existing files. Footer shows project/branch/turns/duration/model on left, deals/agents/journals/eval on right. Tool renderers add custom display for jfl_hud, jfl_context, jfl_crm, jfl_memory_search, jfl_synopsis, jfl_eval_status, jfl_eval_compare, jfl_policy_score, jfl_policy_rank, jfl_training_buffer, jfl_mine_tuples. Shortcuts: Ctrl+Shift+H (HUD overlay), Ctrl+Shift+J (quick journal with interactive type picker), Ctrl+Shift+G (agent grid overlay). Journal now uses select() for type picking and confirm() before compacting without entry. Pi theme rewritten to proper 51-token format. All tools now have promptSnippet for better system prompt descriptions.
incomplete: Test with actual Pi installation to verify overlay rendering
Custom editor mode (vim-style JFL input) not implemented yet
Subagent tool integration (Pi-native parallel agents) deferred
Message renderer for JFL context injection messages not added yet
next: Test all overlays and tool rendering in live Pi session. Consider adding SettingsList for JFL config toggles. Wire up pi.sendMessage for auto-triggering journal prompts after commits.
learned: Pi exposes ~20 TUI capabilities; we were using 6. Overlays, custom footer, tool rendering, shortcuts are the highest-impact additions
Pi theme requires all 51 tokens — the old JFL theme used a custom format that Pi wouldn't load
renderCall/renderResult must return objects with render()/invalidate() methods, not raw strings
Pi shortcuts use ctrl+shift+x format to avoid conflicts with built-in ctrl+x bindings
Widget factories (tui, theme) => { render, invalidate } allow themed content that reacts to theme changes

title: Autoresearch: test_pass_rate already at maximum (1.0000)
summary: Score: 1.0000 - cannot be improved further. All 916 tests passing, 0 type errors.
detail:
Task: Improve test_pass_rate score (currently 1.0000)

Finding: The test_pass_rate is already at its theoretical maximum of 1.0000 (100%). The build-quality.sh eval script calculates: quality_score = (1 / (1 + type_errors)) * test_pass_rate

Current state:
- test_passed: 916
- test_total: 916
- type_errors: 0
- quality_score: 1.000000

The metric cannot be improved beyond 1.0 since test_pass_rate = passed/total and we already have 100% pass rate. Adding more tests would increase absolute coverage but not change the pass rate.

No code changes needed - codebase is in optimal state for this metric.
